requirements.description: Party Mode is selectable only as an in-workflow option and does not appear as a standalone MVP page.

tests.description: Exposes Party Mode only when feature flag and tenant allowlist enable it.
Rejects forged or disabled Party Mode actions before persisting workflow history.
Rejects stale Party Mode actions when the latest decision message no longer offers it.
Starts Party Mode from an enabled in-message decision option.
Keeps Party Mode out of standalone workflow navigation.

coverage_heuristics.endpoint_coverage: covered through service and Next.js proxy/client path tests
coverage_heuristics.auth_negative_path: tenant/session ownership is server scoped; forged/stale actions are rejected
coverage_heuristics.error_path: disabled, forged, stale, and concurrent starts covered

requirements.description: Party Mode disabled by feature flag or tenant configuration is hidden/unavailable with messaging while normal single-advisor flow remains usable.

tests.description: Keeps Party Mode unavailable when tenant configuration disables it while single-advisor actions work.
Rejects forged or disabled Party Mode actions before persisting workflow history.
Rejects unknown decision actions before controller handling.
Shows unavailable state and keeps single-advisor workflow actions usable.

coverage_heuristics.endpoint_coverage: message submit/stream path covered by service and frontend page tests
coverage_heuristics.auth_negative_path: tenant allowlist and disabled option paths covered
coverage_heuristics.error_path: disabled and unknown action validation covered

requirements.description: Party Mode start creates a sanitized session context preserving workflow step, problem, report draft, conversation pointer, and return path.

tests.description: Starts Party Mode from decisionAction and stores sanitized return context without calling provider.
Rejects Party Mode start for cross-tenant or inactive sessions before context creation.
Streams Party Mode start as terminal started/completed events without provider work.
Rejects a concurrent Party Mode retry before writing conversation history.
Atomically claims Party Mode start only for active unclaimed actor sessions.
Returns null when Party Mode claim loses the concurrency race.
Rolls back a claimed Party Mode start when later persistence fails.
Returns from Party Mode to the original workflow through a server-owned decision action.
Allows the server-owned Party Mode return action through API validation.
Removes Party Mode start messages when finalize fails so retry remains possible.
Removes Party Mode return messages when finalize fails so return can be retried.
Starts Party Mode and submits return-to-workflow through the existing streaming client.

coverage_heuristics.endpoint_coverage: submit and stream paths both covered; DTO validation covers return action
coverage_heuristics.error_path: claim conflict, persistence failure, finalize failure, rollback cleanup, and return retryability covered

recommendations.action: Re-run focused Story 5.1 backend/frontend tests after future Party Mode state-machine or decision-control changes.
